Title: The rice GERMINATION DEFECTIVE 1, encoding a B3 domain transcriptional repressor, regulates seed germination and seedling development by integrating GA and carbohydrate metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Panicle (GA4); leaves and stem (starch and soluble sugars)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GA promotes seed germination and seedling development; starch and soluble sugars function as energy and structural carbon reserves for growth and germination
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3 domain transcriptional repressor; VAL-family-like protein; PHD domain; B3 DNA-binding domain; CW-type zinc finger; nuclear localization signal; EAR motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in roots, flowers, stems, leaves and calli; highest in leaf blades and flowers; induced by GA3 and sucrose, slightly induced by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in roots, flowers, stems, leaves and calli; highest in leaf blades and flowers; induced by GA3 and sucrose, slightly induced by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 treatment; ABA treatment; 6% sucrose treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA homeostasis; carbohydrate metabolism; repression of seed maturation program during germination
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via EAR motif and direct DNA binding to RY elements
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses OsLFL1 expression by binding its promoter RY element; likely autorepresses GD1; positively associated with GA biosynthetic gene expression and negatively associated with OsGA2ox3 expression indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: GA induces GD1 expression; GD1 binds its own promoter suggesting autoregulation; participates in GA homeostasis feedback/feedforward network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key repressor integrating GA and carbohydrate metabolism during seed germination and seedling development; loss of GD1 lowers endogenous GA4 and alters starch/sugar balance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GA4 accumulation and starch accumulation while repressing excessive soluble sugar accumulation and seed maturation gene expression; loss of function causes reduced GA4, reduced starch, elevated soluble sugars, and defective germination/dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GD1 encodes a nuclear B3-domain EAR-motif transcriptional repressor required for normal rice seed germination and seedling development. 2. Loss of GD1 decreases endogenous GA4, increases OsGA2ox3 expression, and reduces expression of GA biosynthetic genes including OsGA20ox1, OsGA20ox2 and OsGA3ox2. 3. GD1 directly binds RY elements in the OsLFL1 promoter and represses OsLFL1, linking B3 regulatory networks to GA homeostasis. 4. GD1 expression is induced by GA3 and sucrose, and gd1 mutants show reduced starch but elevated glucose, fructose and sucrose, indicating a role in carbohydrate metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; genomic complementation; qRT-PCR; GA3 rescue assay; endogenous GA4 quantification; transient transcription repression assay in protoplasts; yeast assay; GFP subcellular localization; EMSA for RY binding; carbohydrate metabolite measurements
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GD1 genomic promoter in complementation construct; Actin1 promoter present in T-DNA activation tag; promoter for OsLFL1 overexpression not explicitly stated
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of GD1 causes severe germination defects, dwarfism, abnormal floral development, pollen abortion and high lethality; ectopic perturbation may compromise normal development
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: NA in rice GD1 experimentally; paper notes Arabidopsis VAL/HSI2 PHD domain implication in H3K27 trimethylation as background context only
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate seed germination vigor and early seedling growth through GA/carbohydrate homeostasis, but manipulation requires caution because strong loss or misexpression causes severe dwarfism, sterility and developmental defects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct in vivo ChIP evidence for GD1-OsLFL1 interaction was attempted but unsuccessful due to instability of GD1 protein; regulation of GA metabolic genes appears partly indirect

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GA promotes seed germination and seedling development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3 domain transcription factor; LEC2/FUS3-like protein
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in gd1 mutant; overexpression lines generated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in gd1 mutant; overexpression lines generated
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA homeostasis; seed maturation-related transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts downstream of GD1; overexpression alters expression of GA metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression induces OsGA2ox3 and non-specific LTP2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Downstream B3 regulator whose derepression contributes to reduced GA status and gd1-like dwarf phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of GA deactivation-associated gene OsGA2ox3 and contributes to dwarfism consistent with reduced GA homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLFL1 transcript is significantly upregulated in the gd1 mutant. 2. GD1 directly binds the OsLFL1 promoter RY element, indicating direct repression. 3. OsLFL1 overexpression partly phenocopies gd1, causing dwarfism and small leaves. 4. Overexpression of OsLFL1 induces OsGA2ox3 and non-specific LTP2, supporting its downstream role in GD1-mediated GA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter EMSA via GD1 B3 domain; transgenic overexpression phenotyping; expression analysis in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression promoter in pCAMBIA2300 not explicitly stated
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes dwarf stature and small leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a cautionary downstream regulator; elevated expression is associated with reduced growth and likely undesirable for agronomic performance unless finely tuned
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because it is directly tested in results as a downstream mediator of GD1 and affects GA-related phenotype
